title: REFEREE_DISCOVERY_REQUEST
description: Request sent to any endpoint capable of responding with REFEREE_PROFILE objects. The recipient may be a Keeper node (which naturally discovers Referees through anchoring records), a Norm-managed registry, or any other discovery-capable endpoint. The Claimant chooses the starting point. The recipient responds with REFEREE_DISCOVERY_RESULT (referee_discovery_result.json) carrying the matching REFEREE_PROFILE objects.

filters.description: Filter conditions for Referees. Omitted fields are treated as unconstrained.

jurisdiction_code.description: Required jurisdiction code that the Referee must support (e.g., JP, EU)

domain.description: Required domain that the Referee must support (e.g., automotive, medical)

min_assessments.description: Minimum number of assessments completed. Used as a sample size criterion for evaluating statistical reliability of the Referee's track record.

availability_status.description: If specified, returns only Referees with that status. Typically set to AVAILABLE.

network.description: Target network. Defaults to MAINNET if omitted. Additional network types are not defined by this version of the protocol; extensions are left to community discussion.

fee_model.description: If specified, returns only Referees with that fee model.

max_fee.description: Maximum acceptable fee amount. Requires fee_currency to be specified. Bounded to at most 3 decimal places (every ISO 4217 currency, including BHD/KWD/OMR) so JCS number serialization round-trips exactly across implementations.

fee_currency.description: Currency code for max_fee comparison (ISO 4217, e.g., USD, JPY). Required when max_fee is specified.

referee_id.description: If specified, returns only the profile of this specific Referee.

limit.description: Maximum number of Referees to include in the response
